title: Extract shared _validate_project_root and remove trap defaults
analysis:
Three related changes: (1) Extract _validate_project_root from tools.py into a shared utils/validation.py module with two variants — dict-returning for MCP tools and raising for internal code. (2) Remove the trap `= ''` default from project_root in targeted.py's reconcile_task() and reconcile_bulk_tasks() — the empty string always triggers the fallback-to-project_id path which is now dead code, so making it required surfaces errors at type-check time. (3) Update both tools.py and targeted.py to import from the shared module.

Currently tools.py:223-230 has `_validate_project_root()` returning `dict[str,str]|None`, used by 11 task-related MCP tools via walrus operator. targeted.py:85-87 and 353-354 have inline fallback logic (`if not project_root: project_root = project_id`) which silently masks missing values. All three callers in task_interceptor.py already pass project_root explicitly, so the `= ''` default is unreachable in production — it's only triggered by tests that omit it. 15 existing test calls in test_targeted.py don't pass project_root and rely on the default — these must be updated to pass an explicit absolute path before removing the default. Also co-extracting _validate_project_id (tools.py:214-221) since it follows the same pattern and keeps the shared module complete.

steps.description: Write tests for shared validators in new test_validation.py: (a) validate_project_root — returns error dict for empty string, relative path, None-cast; returns None for valid absolute path like '/home/user/project'. (b) validate_project_id — returns error dict for empty string; returns None for valid id. (c) require_project_root — raises ValueError for empty string, relative path; returns None for valid absolute path. Tests will FAIL because utils/validation.py doesn't exist yet.

Create utils/validation.py with three functions: (1) validate_project_root(project_root: str) -> dict[str,str] | None — core condition `not project_root or not os.path.isabs(project_root)`, returns error dict with 'error' and 'error_type' keys. (2) validate_project_id(project_id: str) -> dict[str,str] | None — checks `not project_id`. (3) require_project_root(project_root: str) -> None — same condition as validate_project_root but raises ValueError. All test_validation.py tests pass.

Refactor tools.py: replace local _validate_project_root (lines 223-230) and _validate_project_id (lines 214-221) with imports from fused_memory.utils.validation. Add `from fused_memory.utils.validation import validate_project_id, validate_project_root` near top. Remove the two local function definitions. All 11 task-tool call sites (`if err := validate_project_root(...)`) and 9+ memory-tool call sites (`if err := validate_project_id(...)`) keep the same walrus-operator pattern — only the import source changes. No behavior change. Existing test_health_endpoint.py tests continue to pass.

Update test_targeted.py in two parts: (a) Add explicit project_root='/tmp/test-project' to all 15 existing test calls that currently omit it — these are at lines 80-82, 95-97, 111-113, 130-132, 148-151, 166-169, 183-186, 196-205, 219-222, 233-236, 352-354, 368-370, 392-394, 415-417, 432-434. This is backward-compatible with current code. (b) Add two new test functions: test_reconcile_task_rejects_invalid_project_root (calls with project_root='' and project_root='relative/path', expects ValueError) and test_reconcile_bulk_tasks_rejects_invalid_project_root (same pattern). New tests will FAIL because targeted.py still has fallback logic instead of raising.

Update targeted.py: (1) Add import `from fused_memory.utils.validation import require_project_root` at top. (2) Remove `= ''` default from project_root parameter in reconcile_task() signature (line 82) and reconcile_bulk_tasks() signature (line 350). (3) Replace inline fallback logic at lines 85-87 (`if not project_root: project_root = project_id`) with `require_project_root(project_root)`. (4) Replace inline fallback at lines 353-354 with same call. All tests now pass — both the 15 updated existing tests and the 2 new validation tests.

design_decisions.rationale: MCP tools must return error dicts to the client (can't raise). Internal code (reconciliation) should raise to fail-fast. Sharing the core condition (`not project_root or not os.path.isabs(project_root)`) avoids drift between the two validation paths.

design_decisions.decision: Place shared validators in new fused_memory/utils/validation.py package
design_decisions.rationale: No existing utils/ package exists. validation.py is a natural home for validators used across server/ and reconciliation/. The utils/ package can later house other cross-cutting utilities.
tools.py:214-221 has a parallel _validate_project_id helper with the same pattern. Extracting both keeps the validation module complete and avoids a half-done refactor.

design_decisions.decision: Update 15 existing tests to pass project_root BEFORE changing targeted.py signature
design_decisions.rationale: These tests currently rely on the `= ''` default which triggers fallback to project_id. Adding explicit project_root is backward-compatible (passes with both old and new code), isolating the behavior change to the new validation-specific tests.

design_decisions.decision: Use '/tmp/test-project' as project_root in updated existing tests
design_decisions.rationale: These tests mock the taskmaster so the path doesn't need to exist on disk. Using a clear, short absolute path satisfies the validator and keeps tests readable. Tests that already use '/home/leo/src/dark-factory' (the separation tests at line 248+) are left as-is.

design_decisions.decision: Remove the fallback-to-project_id logic entirely rather than validating then falling back
design_decisions.rationale: All three callers in task_interceptor.py already pass project_root explicitly. The fallback path is dead code in production — only reached by tests that omit the parameter. Making project_root required is the cleaner contract.

reuse.how: Extract the exact condition (`not project_root or not os.path.isabs(project_root)`) and error dict format into shared module. The dict variant is a direct lift with the underscore prefix removed.
Extract alongside project_root validator. Same lift pattern.
Keep the existing `if err := validate_project_root(project_root): return err` pattern at all call sites — only the import source changes.
Reuse journal, mock_memory_service, mock_taskmaster, config, mock_event_buffer, and reconciler fixtures for new validation tests in the same file.
